summary: PR #2459 matched `lbHeap_80015DF8` in `src/melee/lb/lbheap.c` by preserving an apparently unused extra `OSReport` vararg: the format string consumes `p->size / 1024`, while `p->size` is still passed to reproduce target codegen.

key_files.role: Only changed source file; changed final `OSReport` call in `lbHeap_80015DF8` to pass `p->size` as an extra argument.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: `OSReport(" / %5d KB\n", p->size / 1024)` -> `OSReport(" / %5d KB\n", p->size / 1024, p->size)`

what_changed.text: Added `p->size` as a second vararg to an `OSReport(" / %5d KB\n", ...)` call in `lbHeap_80015DF8`.
The PR body says the format string only consumes the divided KB value, but keeping the extra vararg matches target codegen.
The PR claims `lbHeap_80015DF8` improved from 99.87654% to 100.0%, with function size remaining 324/324 bytes.
A bot report recorded one new match for `main/melee/lb/lbheap` item `lbHeap_80015DF8`, with matched code +324 bytes.

smart_moves.text: Kept the change minimal: one argument added to one report call, no broad formatting, naming, data, or split churn.
Documented the non-obvious reason for an apparently unused vararg so reviewers can distinguish it from accidental dead or mismatched formatting code.
Provided narrow verification commands for the touched object, objdiff unit, and issue lint/report tool.

decomp_lessons.lesson: For variadic reporting calls such as `OSReport`, do not assume every source-level argument must be consumed by the format string; an extra vararg may be needed to match target codegen when verified by objdiff.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text: extra `p->size` vararg matches target codegen

decomp_lessons.lesson: When a one-line vararg change closes a function, record the exact before/after score and object/unit verification because the source shape may look suspicious without that context.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Preserve an extra unused-looking vararg in `OSReport` to reproduce the original call sequence/codegen.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.caution: This PR supports the tactic only for the specific `lbHeap_80015DF8` call with stated objdiff verification; it should not be generalized to arbitrary report calls without local evidence.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Use narrow touched-object and objdiff commands for a focused one-function match.

review_feedback.text: decomp-dev bot reported 1 new match: `main/melee/lb/lbheap` / `lbHeap_80015DF8`, before 99.88%, after 100.00%, +324 bytes.

follow_up_queries: OSReport extra vararg match target codegen
variadic report call unused argument decomp matching

notes: The slice contains PR metadata, the complete small diff, changed-file metadata, PR body, and bot issue comment.
No human review comments are present in the loaded slice.
Verification results are taken from the PR author's stated commands and bot report; intake did not rerun builds or objdiff.
The PR body asserts original-code intent for the extra argument; intake records that assertion as PR evidence, not independently proven historical author intent.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Verified unused-looking varargs can be required for variadic report-call matches
accepted_candidate_records.text: In PR #2459, `lbHeap_80015DF8` reached 100% by passing `p->size` as an extra `OSReport` argument even though the format string only consumes `p->size / 1024`. Treat such shapes as acceptable only with narrow codegen/objdiff evidence.

source_update_candidates.title: Mention verified extra varargs for variadic report calls
source_update_candidates.text: Consider adding a note under report/assert or matching tactics standards: for variadic calls like `OSReport`, an argument not consumed by the visible format string can still be original and match-critical, but should remain only when documented with narrow objdiff/build evidence.
source_update_candidates.reason: The existing standards cover preserving real OSReport behavior and evidence-backed matching tactics, but this PR provides a concrete recurring-looking edge case for variadic report calls.

rejection_notes: Do not infer that all extra varargs on `OSReport` calls are acceptable; the PR only supports this specific call with stated verification.
Do not promote the PR author's phrase 'original code passes' into independently proven original-source intent beyond this PR's evidence.
No naming convention was extracted because the PR did not rename symbols or locals.
